frameworks.name: structural alignment
frameworks.one_line: prior relational schema (mental line) is re-used to organize newly learned reward associations

frameworks.name: model-based reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: probabilistic reward learning task supplies the new value structure being mapped

frameworks.name: transfer learning
frameworks.one_line: knowledge of one ordered domain (numbers) transfers to a novel ordered domain (donkeys)

core_question: Do humans repurpose pre-existing ordinal mental representations (e.g., the number line) to encode newly learned reward-probability rankings in the brain?
key_assumptions: ordinal information is represented along a shared mental line across domains
reward-probability rank can be acquired through trial-and-error feedback
scalp EEG patterns can index domain-general ordinal representations
manipulations: stimulus type viewed (six colored donkeys vs digits 1–6)
reward probability associated with each donkey (5% to 95% across six levels)
key_findings: participants learned to rank donkeys by reward probability through trial and error
EEG patterns evoked by each donkey resembled the EEG pattern for the digit at the matching rank
the rank–to–rank correspondence held across the full six-item ordering
interpretations: newly learned reward structure is encoded by re-using a pre-existing ordinal (number-line) representation
learning exploits relational knowledge across stimuli rather than treating items as isolated associations
